section.name: shared-services
section.summary: Shared vs isolated decision matrix for PostgreSQL and Redis, per-project database setup, and Redis DB number allocation.

rule.statement: For solo developers running 2-5 projects on one VPS, use a single shared PostgreSQL instance with separate databases per project, and a single Redis with separate DB numbers or key prefixes.
rule.rationale: A solo VPS with 30GB RAM can run one well-tuned PostgreSQL instance versus four independent instances, each consuming 500MB+ for shared_buffers alone. Shared services cost less RAM and have a single backup target. The blast radius of a single shared instance failure is acceptable when one developer can fix it immediately.

list.item: Shared: lower RAM and disk consumption, single backup, simpler monitoring
Shared: appropriate for solo dev at 2-5 projects with acceptable isolation
Isolated: required for compliance, customer data segregation, or team environments
Isolated: appropriate when one project needs a different PostgreSQL major version
Isolated: appropriate when a single project justifies dedicated hosting costs

section.name: postgresql-per-project

rule.statement: Create a separate database and a dedicated database user per project; never share credentials between projects.
rule.rationale: Separate users mean a compromised project cannot query another project's database. Separate databases make per-project backups and restores possible without touching other projects.

section.reference: Script: create PostgreSQL database + user in shared Docker instance with auto-generated password

list.item: DB number allocation: DB 0 = project 1, DB 1 = project 1 Celery, DB 2 = project 2, DB 3 = project 3
Key prefix alternative: nero:cache:*, nero:sessions:*, mtax:cache:* (all in DB 0)
DB numbers are simpler to reason about; key prefixes are more flexible but require discipline
Redis ships with 16 databases (0-15) by default — sufficient for up to 8 projects with 2 DBs each
PostgreSQL shared_buffers: set to 25% of RAM allocated to postgres (e.g., 2GB for an 8GB budget)
Redis maxmemory: set to prevent Redis from consuming all available RAM on cache growth
Per-service MemoryMax in systemd units: hard limit that triggers OOM kill before system OOM
Signal to split to a second server: consistent memory pressure above 85% for 7+ days
